title: Where the AI Discourse Won't Look
hook: When the work gets easy, what happens to you?
desc: A conversation with a computer-science professor reveals the question the AI debate never reaches: every concern is a “what do we do” lever, and the missing question is “what are we for.”
img: Episode 1 The Conversation About the Conversation with Dr. Dan NO TEXT.png

title: What Are We For?
hook: Who would notice if you started to disappear?
desc: The novella Therapist — a system that does everything right and is pointed at the wrong thing. Behavior versus orientation, and AI as the amplifier of an absence of human connection.
img: Episode 2 What Are We For - NO TEXT.png

title: Two Men in Two Cells
hook: Where do you go to ask the questions that actually matter?
desc: Socrates in his cell at dawn and a man named PT at a screen, asking the same question — and the public square that has been quietly removed from modern life.
img: Episode 3 - Two Men in Two Cells - NO TEXT.png

title: The Final Cause
hook: What would it actually mean for you to flourish?
desc: Aristotle's four causes and the final cause (telos), and the empty institutional slot where the answer to “what is AI for” is supposed to live. Introduces eudaimonia.
img: Episode 4 - The Final Cause - NO TEXT.png

title: F=ma?
hook: What do you take on faith and call it science?
desc: An engineer on what science actually is from the inside, the gap between that and what the culture made of it, and the cost of making a god of science.

title: The Criterion of Truth
hook: What's the one thing you couldn't be talked out of?
desc: Agrippa's trilemma and how anyone knows anything is true at all — and the one thing the skeptics could never take away.
img: Episode 6 - The Criterion of Truth - Canva - NO TEXT.png
